title: S3 Storage Read and Delete Pipeline
read_flow: START: read({ location, range, version_id }) called by Caller
Build GetObjectCommand params: { Bucket: bucket, Key: key, [Range: range], [VersionId: version_id] }
client.send(GetObjectCommand) → AWS S3 responds with response object
Readable.from(response.Body) → convert Body to Node.js Readable stream
delete_flow: START: delete({ node }) called by Caller
await node.get('s3:location') → node_storage { bucket_region, bucket, key }
Build DeleteObjectCommand params: { Bucket: node_storage.bucket, Key: node_storage.key }

method_signatures.read: async read({ location: { bucket_region, bucket, key }, range?, version_id? }) → Readable

key_conditions: read: optional range → adds Range header to GetObjectCommand
read: optional version_id → adds VersionId to GetObjectCommand
delete: node location resolved dynamically via node.get('s3:location') (async metadata lookup)
dependencies: delete depends_on node.get('s3:location') for bucket metadata
important_notes: No retry logic in read() or delete() — retries only in upload path. read() returns a raw Readable stream (no buffering). delete() resolves bucket info lazily from the node metadata abstraction.
